rag_rules: AGREE annex elements must not conflict with component type feature names; resolve naming collisions by moving conflicting elements to implementation scope or renaming them.
AGREE contracts in component type annexes must only reference visible ports and features; avoid referencing subcomponent paths or implementation-only elements.
generic_rules: AGREE annexes must be placed in appropriate AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
AGREE expressions must use only visible identifiers or locally declared helpers; avoid referencing undefined or package-qualified names.
AGREE guarantees must relate visible inputs to visible outputs; do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths in type-level contracts.
AGREE scalar type declarations must use int, real, or bool; avoid AADL property names or invalid type references.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks with valid syntax: assume, guarantee, eq, const, assign.
Component type annexes are for assumptions and guarantees involving visible ports; implementation annexes are for assignments and bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assignments or subcomponent references in component type annexes; move them to implementation scope or remove them.
Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) as AGREE variables; these are not automatically available in AGREE scope.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 1
